title: end
text: end Marks the end of a ProcScript module, operation, or trigger. end Return Values None. The end statement does not affect the value of $status . Use Allowed in all Uniface component types. Description The end statement indicates the end of a ProcScript module or operation. If this statement is encountered during execution, an implicit done is executed and the module or operation ends, returning the current value of $status . If an function or operation statement or the end of the trigger is encountered, an end (and implicit done ) for the previous ProcScript module or operation is assumed. ProcScript statements after an end are not recognized unless they are part of another ProcScript module or operation. That is, if ProcScript statements following an end are meant to be executed, the statement immediately following the end should be a function or operation statement. Labels defined with blockdata statements must be defined at the end of a ProcScript module or operation, before the end statement is encountered. The following example shows the use of end for a trigger and a function statement: trigger store call LSTORE end ; end store ; container: Local ProcScript Modules (component-level) function LSTORE store if ($status < 0) message "Store error!" rollback else message "Store done." commit endif end ; end LSTORE function Copy Example: Defining an Operation The following example shows the operation DISCOUNT of a service component named SERV1: operation DISCOUNT params string CUSTID : IN numeric AMOUNT : INOUT numeric PERCENTAGE : OUT endparams ; no discount till proven otherwise ; 20% discount for Uniface ; 15% discount for Acme ; adjust amount PERCENTAGE = 0 if ( CUSTID == "ufbv" ) PERCENTAGE = 20 if ( CUSTID == "acme" ) PERCENTAGE = 15 AMOUNT = AMOUNT * ( 100 - PERCENTAGE) / 100 end Copy The operation DISCOUNT could be referenced from another component as follows: activate "SERV1".DISCOUNT (ID.CUST, TOTAL.INVOICE, $DISCOUNT$) Copy Related Links entry Declare a ProcScript entry, a script module that can be invoked from within the same component. operation Declare an operation ProcScript module. function...end Declare a ProcScript function, a script module that can be invoked from other modules in its vicinity.
